free_text: -NUCLIDE YIELDS OF LIGHT FISSION PRODUCTS FROM THERMAL
NEUTRON INDUCED FISSION OF 233-U AT DIFFERENT KINETIC
F.G., J.P., H.S., G.S. AND EXPERIMENTAL SITE.
A HIGH-FLUX REACTOR, DELIVERING A THERMAL
NEUTRON FLUX OF 5.0E14 NEUTRONS/CM2 AND SECOND ONTO
THE ILL RECOIL SPECTROMETER LOHENGRIN, WHERE
THE FISSION PRODUCTS ARE SELECTED IN FLIGHT
ACCORDING TO THE MAGNETIC AND ELECTRIC FIELD SETTINGS,
AN IONIZATION CHAMBER WAS USED TO DETECT THE
.THE SAMPLE CONSISTED OF A 40 MICRO-G/CM2 LAYER OF
233-U IN THE FORM OF U3O8 ON A TITANIUM BACKING. THE
SAMPLE WAS COVERED WITH A TA LAYER OF 140 MICRO-G/CM2
THICKNESS. THE ENRICHMENT OF 233-U WAS 99.439 PERCENT.
THE SIZE OF THE SAMPLE WAS 40 X 3 MM, AND THE BURNUP
.THE FRAGMENT ENERGIES WERE CORRECTED FOR THE LOSSES IN
THE SAMPLE MATERIAL. THE MASS YIELDS WERE CORRECTED
FOR THE MASS AND ENERGY DISPERSION OF THE SPECTROME-
TER, FOR THE BURNUP OF THE SAMPLE, AS WELL AS FOR THE
SMALL LONG-TERM POWER FLUCTUATIONS OF THE REACTOR.
S.W. REACTION CORRECTED IN SUBENTS 4 AND 5,
S.W. REACTION MODIFIED IN SUBENTS 2,3 AND 6.

NEUTRON CHAIN YIELDS NORMALISED TO A TOTAL OF 100.
ARE NORMALIZED TO 100 FOR EACH ENERGY.
INDEPENDENT YIELDS NORMALIZED TO UNITY FOR EACH MASS
